title: Discriminative Active Learning (DAL)
text_0: **Idea:** DAL selects samples that make the labeled set better match the unlabeled distribution. It trains a binary discriminator to distinguish labeled vs unlabeled (typically in feature space) and queries the unlabeled points the discriminator rates most unlabeled-like, thus covering underrepresented regions. It does not use predictive uncertainty such that effectiveness hinges on the representation and discriminator calibration.
